industries.name: Ionization Smoke Detectors
uses: Americium-241 (²⁴¹Am, t½ = 432 years) in ionization smoke detectors: a tiny sealed ²⁴¹Am source (~1 μCi, 0.9 μg) ionizes air between two electrodes, creating a small current. Smoke particles entering the chamber reduce ionization current, triggering the alarm

Ionization smoke detectors are far more responsive to fast-flaming fires (paper, wood with open flame) than photoelectric detectors — the two technologies are complementary and building codes in many jurisdictions require both
Approximately 40,000 kg of americium-241 is used annually in smoke detectors globally — making it one of the most widely distributed radioactive materials in everyday consumer products

²⁴¹Am is produced from plutonium-241 (²⁴¹Pu) beta decay in spent nuclear fuel — it accumulates in aged plutonium stockpiles and is extracted during reprocessing. The US, Russia, UK, and France are primary producers

Am-241 smoke detector recycling: the Nuclear Regulatory Commission requires proper disposal. In practice, americium is difficult to recover economically from discarded detectors due to the very small per-unit quantity (~1μCi)

companies.name: Kidde (Carrier Global)
companies.use: World's largest smoke detector manufacturer. Produces ionization smoke alarms containing Am-241 sources for residential, commercial, and industrial fire detection globally. Major consumer of sealed Am-241 ionization sources.

companies.name: First Alert (BRK Brands)
companies.use: Major smoke alarm manufacturer producing Am-241 ionization alarms for residential and commercial use. Also produces combination ionization/photoelectric dual-sensor alarms meeting the latest fire safety recommendations.

companies.name: Orano / ASNL (Australian Nuclear Science)
companies.use: Produces and supplies sealed Am-241 ionization sources to smoke detector manufacturers worldwide. Americium-241 is extracted from aged plutonium metal as ²⁴¹Pu beta-decays to ²⁴¹Am in plutonium stockpiles.

industries.name: Industrial Gauging & Medical
uses: Am-241 gamma sources (59.5 keV) for industrial thickness and density gauging: level sensors in tanks, paper and plastic film thickness measurement, and soil density gauges used in road construction compaction testing

Am-Be (americium-beryllium) neutron sources: ²⁴¹Am alpha particles on beryllium produce neutrons (~2×10⁷ n/s per Ci of Am-241) — used in oil well logging, neutron activation analysis, nuclear reactor startup sources, and laboratory neutron sources
Am-241 in bone density measurement (DEXA scan sources) — being replaced by X-ray tube sources in modern DXA systems

Americium-242m (²⁴²ᵐAm) theoretical nuclear fuel: has an extremely low critical mass (~9g) making it the most efficient fissile nuclide by mass — purely theoretical due to production difficulty and cost

companies.name: Eckert & Ziegler
companies.use: Major producer of sealed Am-241 sources for industrial gauging, Am-Be neutron sources, and smoke detector ionization elements. Supplies licensed Am-241 sources to level gauge manufacturers, oil well logging companies, and nuclear instrumentation suppliers.

companies.name: Halliburton (Sperry Drilling)
companies.use: Major oil well logging company using Am-Be neutron sources in its nuclear logging tools to measure formation porosity, water saturation, and lithology in oil and gas exploration wells globally.

industries.name: Space Power (Emerging)
uses: Americium-241 as alternative to plutonium-238 for space RTGs: Am-241's longer half-life (432 years vs 87.7 for ²³⁸Pu) means lower power density but more stable long-term output — suitable for very long missions (100+ years) and lower-power applications

ESA (European Space Agency) developing Am-241 RTG for future deep space missions as Europe has no access to ²³⁸Pu — the UK's National Nuclear Laboratory is extracting Am-241 from aged UK plutonium stockpiles for ESA's space power program
Am-241 European Space Power (ASRG): ESA's program to develop a 50W Am-241 RTG for the Cosmic Vision and Voyage 2050 deep space science missions requiring radioisotope power beyond solar panel range

companies.name: National Nuclear Laboratory (NNL)
companies.use: Extracting americium-241 from the UK's aged civil plutonium stockpile at Sellafield for ESA's space RTG development program. Producing gram quantities of Am-241 in high purity form for conversion to AmO₂ fuel for RTG heat source pellets.
